input:
Dear Editor,

I am writing to share my story in the hopes that it will help others who may be considering suicide.

I am a senior citizen and I have been struggling with mental health issues for many years. A few years ago, I decided to take my own life by jumping off a construction site.

Thankfully, I was unsuccessful and did not die. However, I was severely injured and spent many months in the hospital recovering.

Since then, I have been through a lot of therapy and I am now in a much better place. I want to share my story in the hopes that it will help someone who is thinking about suicide to realize that there is hope.

If you or someone you know is considering suicide, please seek help from a mental health professional. There is always help available.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
label: APPROVED

>>> Title: Life Skills for Young Adults: What They Don't Teach You in High School - 25 Essential Skills Every College Student Should Learn

>>> Content:you have some “me time” to do something you enjoy.
8. Be assertive – say “no” to things that you don’t want to do or that will cause you undue stress.
9. Learn to accept the things you can’t change – this can be difficult, but holding onto resentment and frustration will only add to your stress levels.
10. Seek professional help – if your stress levels are impacting your day-to-day life, it may be time to seek help from a therapist or counselor.

Stress is a normal human emotion, but it’s important to manage it before it begins to impact your health and wellbeing. Identify the things that cause you stress and find healthy ways to let some of the tension out. Exercise, relaxation techniques, spending time with loved ones and doing things you enjoy can all help to reduce stress levels. If your stress is starting to impact your life, seek professional help.
Talk about it!
The more we learn to talk about how we feel, the better our mental health can be. This isn’t a sign of weakness, in fact, it’s empowering as it helps you take control of your own personal wellbeing.
 
We all have disappointments and crisis points in our lives,  so it’s natural to feel down, anxious or stressed from time to time. It’s only when these feelings become prolonged and begin to impact your <DateTime> life that they become a problem. Talking about how you feel can be the first step in managing stress, anxiety or depression.
Remember you are not alone. There are many people who feel just like you do and there is no shame in seeking help.  Talk to a trusted friend or family member, your GP, a counselor or therapist. The sooner you reach out for help, the sooner you can begin to feel better.


What is stress?
Stress is our body’s response to demands placed on it. It is a normal human emotion that we all experience from time to time. Stress can be caused by a variety of things, including work, relationships, finances or health. When we feel stressed, our body releases hormones such as adrenaline and cortisol. These hormones prepare our body for “fight or flight” – they increase our

The first step is to talk to someone you trust – a friend, family member, your doctor or a counselor. Talking about how you feel can be very therapeutic and can help you to manage yourYou are not alone in this, and people will be happy to help you. Seek help early and talk through what’s happened and how you feel. Remember to put things into perspective and when the time is right, empower yourself by making a decision and sticking to it. Then move on!


If you’re not sure where to start, here are some tips:
1. Talk to a friend or family member – they may be going through something similar and can offer support and understanding.
2. See your GP – they can give you information about mental health conditions and refer you to other services if necessary.
3. Call a helpline – such as <Organization>

Chapter 6: Personal Healthcare & Basic First Aid
It’s important to take care of your health and wellbeing, both physically and mentally. This chapter will give you some tips on how to do that. We’ll also cover some basic first aid so that you can be prepared in case of an emergency.

How to perform basic first aid
First aid is the help given to someone who has been injured or who is suddenly ill. It is emergency care given before the arrival of ambulance paramedics or other medical help.

Basic first aid involves four steps:
1. Protection – ensuring that you and the casualty are safe from further harm
2. Preserving life – can include resusc

input: It’s commonly accepted as fact that the horror genre has frequently portrayed queer individuals (along with women and minorities) in a less than flattering light, and it’s all too easy to see why: films like DRESSED TO KILL (1980) and SILENCE OF THE LAMBS (1991) depict transgender people as mentally-unstable murderers, while the bisexual bloodsuckers of VAMPYROS LESBOS (1971) and THE HUNGER (1983) exist solely for the titillation of the straight male viewer.
